calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Order Compelling Arbitration
Off Calendar Apr-06-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
John Brian McCauley, 415-974-1515 of J. Brian McCauley, A Professional Law Corporation for Petitioners George Leow, et al.
No other appearances.

Discussed. The Court rules as follows: PETITIONER GEORGE LOEW, JOSEPH MALLON, ANDREW LUSARDI, JANE FRESTON, FLORENCE SURRATT, MARTIN MILLER Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Order Compelling Arbitration is Granted. Order signed. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Order Compelling Arbitration
Off Calendar Mar-30-2021 Continued To Apr-06-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

John Brian McCauley; J. Brian McCauley, A Professional Law Corporation; 415-974-1515; appearing in person on behalf of petitioners George Leow, et al.
Alice M. Traeg; self-represented respondent appearing on her own behalf.

Discussed. Responded contests the tentative ruling, and states that she will face financial hardship if the matter proceeds to arbitration. Respondent states she was unable to file a formal opposition to petitioner's motion due to various harships she is experiencing. The court offers to pass the matter so that the parties can have a discussion outside as to how they would like to proceed. The parties stipulate to a one week continuance so that they can try and resolve their issues informally outside of the court. Both parties waive notice. The court rules as follows: PETITIONERS GEORGE LOEW, JOSEPH MALLON, ANDREW LUSARDI, JANE FRESTON, FLORENCE SURRATT and MARTIN MILLER's Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Order Compelling Arbitration is continued to April 6, 2021. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
